final_review: Git&Run built an innovative platform with features including Next.js frontend, FastAPI backend for handling HTTP requests, Firebase integration for authentication and Firestore database, and an API for user registration and login. The team started with setting up the project structure and dependencies before progressing to adding new functionalities like anomaly detection, fund flow tracking, and reallocation of funds between schemes. They committed frequently but often in large batches, leading to some messy code. The code quality varied; some commits were well-structured and added meaningful features, while others were more setup-oriented or AI-generated. The standout commit was the addition of Firebase services for authentication and Firestore integration. However, there were several wasted commits, including merge commits and empty files that did not add any new functionality. Overall, Git&Run demonstrated a solid understanding of Next.js and FastAPI but struggled with code quality and efficiency. Their project was functional but lacked substantial features that could have made it more impactful in a 24-hour hackathon.
